Continuity revalidation waiver summary

Summary card that should be dropped.

Continuity revalidation waiver FAQ

Continuity revalidation waiver FAQs matter when operators need direct answers about when the default revalidation sequence can be safely bypassed.

The guidance explains how review checkpoints, queue health evidence, and fallback queues should be checked before a waiver answer is reused.

Teams are expected to preserve fairness controls, handoff records, and recovery evidence so waiver decisions remain traceable across shifts.

Longer incidents still require explicit rollback ownership and a fresh approval window before a waiver path is extended.
